game_name: Earth 2150: Escape from the Blue Planet

danger_signals.visual_form: Faction-colored unit models on battlefield map
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring defensive or offensive response

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual updates to mission objectives, enemy unit concentrations
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat or mission complication (e.g., hacker capturing UCS units, alliance breakdown)

danger_signals.visual_form: Transport carriers flying exposed paths, depot locations under fire
danger_signals.meaning: Logistical failure imminent; units will lose ammo and combat effectiveness

danger_signals.visual_form: Terrain changes from frozen → vegetated → desert → lava flows; water bodies evaporate
danger_signals.meaning: World-state degradation; weapon effectiveness shifts, naval units become unusable

danger_signals.visual_form: Unit behavior change after extended combat (e.g., powerful grenade launcher depletes ammo rapidly)
danger_signals.meaning: Unit requires resupply or will become defenseless

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual/mechanical feedback as global temperature rises
danger_signals.meaning: Thermal weapons lose potency; strategy must adapt to world-state

safety_signals.visual_form: Persistent parallel map accessible at will; visible spaceport structure
safety_signals.meaning: Secure rear area for unit production, research, credit storage; evacuation point

safety_signals.visual_form: Transport link structure connecting mission site to main base
safety_signals.meaning: Supply route open; units and credits can be ferried safely; backup reinforcement available

safety_signals.visual_form: Unit level indicators increasing through successful combat
safety_signals.meaning: Unit has survived and grown stronger; increased reliability and combat effectiveness

safety_signals.visual_form: Weapon ammo bar slowly refilling without resupply
safety_signals.meaning: No logistical dependence; unit remains combat-ready indefinitely

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual shield effect on unit (regenerating, energy-based defense)
safety_signals.meaning: Protection against energy weapons; tactical advantage in certain matchups

safety_signals.visual_form: Credit counter at main base increasing; visual feedback when 10,000 threshold reached and launch occurs
safety_signals.meaning: Progress toward evacuation goal; permanent conversion of resources to escape

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: UCS units can be captured and turned against players via scripted hacker events
Distinct unit models and chassis designs differentiating ED technology
Access to weather controller superweapon; unique unit types and strategies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Weather-based environmental effects tied to Lunar Coalition faction presence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Frigid terrain, snow/ice coverage, frozen water bodies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Heat-based weapons function normally; navies operational; standard unit effectiveness

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Global climate phase 2 - Thaw and growth
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ice melts, plants and vegetation grow across landscape, terrain becomes green
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Transition period; heat weapons unchanged; navies still viable; terrain traversability increases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Vegetation dies, deserts overtake terrain, water bodies begin to evaporate
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Heat-based weapons become less effective; naval units increasingly obsolete; terrain becomes sparse

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Global climate phase 4 - Complete environmental collapse
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lava flows glow across landscape, ash coverage, entire oceans gone from globe, barren wasteland
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Heat weapons severely reduced or ineffective; navies completely retired; weather controller actions change; only heat-resistant/adapted units effective

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Minable ore deposits diminished or exhausted on mission map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must decide to commit remaining ore to transport building for evacuation fund or retain for unit construction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Former allied units change faction coloring or behavior; enemy unit concentrations shift
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mission objectives update; previously non-hostile forces become active threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: UI feedback on unit construction limitations; visual queue blockage in factories
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New military unit construction disabled; player must destroy or retire units to build new ones

environmental_storytelling.example: Campaign mission map selection screen shows Earth progressively transitioning from frozen sphere → green/vegetated → brown desert → lava-covered wasteland, communicating existential urgency and time pressure

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Main spaceport base remains constant while mission sites degrade, reinforcing that evacuation is the only viable survival strategy

environmental_storytelling.example: Periodic news updates about evacuation program progress, quota reminders, or faction threats reinforce narrative stakes without interrupting gameplay
Heat-based weapon effectiveness directly tied to global temperature visual state, making environmental storytelling mechanically meaningful
Finite mission ore deposits and spaceport credit thresholds (1,000,000 for main factions, 500,000 for Lunar Coalition) communicate resource desperation and survival economics
Playable ships disappear from available unit roster as water bodies evaporate; visual absence communicates environmental catastrophe more effectively than text

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Flies in straight line unless manually commanded; vulnerable to enemy air defenses if pathed incorrectly, signaling logistical weakness
Ammo depletes rapidly; requires flying supply carrier resupply; different tactical support needs than sustained-fire units
Does no HP damage; heats enemy units until ammo detonates; ammunition effect wears off if interrupted, signaling temporary vulnerability
Heat effect dissipates; requires re-engagement to re-trigger detonation chain, indicating tactical reset opportunity
Extremely high damage output; bypasses armored chassis damage reduction, signaling elite/desperate firepower
Shield regenerates, negating heat/energy weapon effects; signals tactical advantage in energy-dominant scenarios
Changes faction control (visual/mechanical); formerly allied unit now attacks player, signaling mission complication and strategic disruption
Retained in main base; carries combat experience forward; counts toward population cost cap, signaling investment and strategic continuity

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Unit class with extended command set
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Retreats at 50% HP or holds defined area; pursues limited distance, signaling player-defined defensive posture without constant micromanagement

notes: Earth 2150 uses environmental degradation as primary world-state indicator and mechanical consequence. Visual climate progression (frozen → desert → lava) directly affects faction capabilities, weapon effectiveness, and unit viability, creating feedback loop between narrative catastrophe and strategic gameplay. Faction distinction relies primarily on unit model/chassis design rather than explicit color coding (not documented in source). Landing pad vulnerability and supply line logistics add spatial danger signals absent from earlier RTS titles. Spaceport credit threshold mechanic (10,000-credit auto-launch) creates visible economic communication of progress toward evacuation goal.
